model.description: Google • Fast Gemini model optimized for low-latency reasoning, multimodal inputs, and tool use.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) enters this matchup with a superior analytical profile, ranking in the top 35 nationally in both offensive and defensive efficiency. While Missouri benefits from a near-home-court advantage in St. Louis and the return of key guard Jayden Stone, their defensive inconsistencies will likely struggle to contain Miami's balanced attack. The Hurricanes' experience in a competitive ACC and their ability to generate stops more consistently give them the edge in this 7/10 matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Efficiency and Defensive Edge
reasoningStep.summary: Miami (FL) is favored due to a higher overall efficiency ranking and a more balanced profile on both ends of the court compared to Missouri.
evidence: Missouri playing in St. Louis provides a geographic advantage

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

While Missouri benefits from a near-home-court advantage in St. Louis and the return of key guard Jayden Stone, their defensive inconsistencies will likely struggle to contain Miami's balanced attack. The Hurricanes' experience in a competitive ACC and their ability to generate stops more consistently give them the edge in this 7/10 matchup."},"reasoningStep":{"id":"miami-missouri-analysis","title":"Efficiency and Defensive Edge","summary":"Miami (FL) is favored due to a higher overall efficiency ranking and a more balanced profile on both ends of the court compared to Missouri.","evidence":["Miami (FL) KenPom rank: 30, Missouri: 52","Miami AdjOE: 121.4 (34th), Missouri AdjOE: 119.5 (50th)","Miami AdjDE: 100.7, Missouri AdjDE: 104.1","Missouri playing in St. Louis provides a geographic advantage","Jayden Stone returned for Missouri"]}}
